questions.explanation: Re-running the suite per commit is regression testing, giving immediate breakage feedback. (Software Testing p.81)

questions.stem: For a JHotDraw collaborator such as a drawing context or storage layer that a unit depends on, what does the guide advise doing in tests?

options.text: Run the real one and test the GUI
Replace it with a mock/stub via its interface

questions.explanation: Interface-based collaborators are replaced with mocks/stubs to isolate the unit. (Software Testing p.57-75)

questions.stem: What is the test benefit phrased as 'only write production code to make a failing test pass'?
questions.explanation: Writing code only to pass a failing test yields high coverage by construction. (Software Testing p.49)

questions.stem: Within EnqueueTest, what is the job of the @After method tearDown()?

options.text: Empties the queue after each test

questions.explanation: tearDown() (@After) empties the queue after each test. (Software Testing p.52)

questions.stem: In the EnqueueTest, what does setUp() build and why?

options.text: A Mockito mock of the queue
A new CheckRepWrapper(new Queue(2)) so each test starts clean and invariant-checked

questions.explanation: setUp() builds a CheckRepWrapper(Queue(2)) so each test begins with a clean, invariant-checked queue. (Software Testing p.52)

questions.stem: As phrased on the deck's title slide, the goal of testing is which of these?
questions.explanation: The title slide frames testing as 'how to make software fail'. (Software Testing p.1)

questions.stem: What does the deck define as the basic test model's components in order?

options.text: test inputs, SUT, test outputs, oracle

questions.explanation: Inputs -> SUT -> outputs -> oracle. (Software Testing p.7)

questions.stem: For a user who types an invalid password, which statement correctly separates the role of an assertion from that of an exception?

options.text: Use an assertion to reject the password
Use an exception (expected runtime error); an assertion is for 'should never happen'

questions.explanation: Bad input is an expected runtime error handled by an exception; assertions are for impossible states. (Software Testing p.18)

questions.stem: In the deck's ideas-for-tests, which is listed as a source of test ideas?

options.text: Code experiments ('how does the system behave if ...')

questions.explanation: Code experiments ('how does the system behave if ...') are a listed source of test ideas. (Software Testing p.40)

questions.stem: What is the alternative scenario in the Borrow Book use case?

options.text: The user already has 10 books borrowed, so the system shows an error

questions.explanation: The alternative scenario: the user already has 10 books -> the system shows an error. (Software Testing p.41)

questions.stem: Once the main scenario is green, what outcome does the alternative-scenario test expect?

options.text: A TooManyBooksException when borrowing the 11th book

questions.explanation: It expects TooManyBooksException after 10 books are already borrowed. (Software Testing p.45)

questions.stem: What does Mockito.times(2) inside verify assert?

options.text: The method was called exactly twice

questions.explanation: times(2) asserts exactly two invocations. (Software Testing p.71)

questions.stem: What is the white-box test testEnqueueTail() best described as?

options.text: A path-targeted test that drives the tail==max -> 0 path

questions.explanation: testEnqueueTail() targets the wrap-around path to assert getTail()==0. (Software Testing p.13)

questions.stem: What is the role of the specification slide for choosing tests?

options.text: It defines the equivalence classes and therefore which tests must exist

questions.explanation: The spec's domain to range defines equivalence classes and the required tests. (Software Testing p.23)

questions.stem: Which best describes 'differential testing's oracle'?

options.text: Another (reference) implementation whose output is compared with the SUT's

questions.explanation: Differential testing's oracle is a reference implementation. (Software Testing p.8)

questions.stem: What is the consequence of treating a green suite as proof of correctness?

options.text: False confidence; residual bugs in untested paths go unnoticed

questions.explanation: Believing green = correct gives false confidence; bugs hide in untested paths. (Software Testing p.6)

questions.stem: In the deck's testing-kinds map, where do differential, stress, and random testing sit?

options.text: They are sub-levels of unit testing
They cut across the unit/integration/system levels

questions.explanation: Differential, stress, and random testing cut across the three levels. (Software Testing p.8)

questions.stem: What specifically makes differential testing so powerful in a maintenance setting?

options.text: The previous release provides a free, ready-made oracle

questions.explanation: In maintenance the prior release is a free oracle for the new version. (Software Testing p.8)

questions.stem: What is the relationship between equivalence partitioning and the impossibility result?

options.text: Partitioning gives adequate finite coverage of an infinite domain, addressing the impossibility of a perfect suite

questions.explanation: Partitioning provides justified finite coverage of an infinite domain, the practical response to impossibility. (Software Testing p.12, p.5)

questions.stem: In the Spring + JUnit example, what kind of dependency wiring is demonstrated?

options.text: Dependency injection of beans by the Spring container into the test

questions.explanation: Spring's DI container wires beans into the JUnit test via @Autowired. (Software Testing p.76)

questions.stem: What is the most appropriate double when you need a simplified but working implementation, like an in-memory database?
questions.explanation: A fake is a working but simplified implementation such as an in-memory DB. (Software Testing p.60, +[Raj13])

questions.stem: What is a dummy object in the broader test-double taxonomy?

options.text: An object passed but never actually used

questions.explanation: A dummy is passed around but never used (taxonomy beyond the named mock/stub/spy). (Software Testing p.60, +[Raj13])

questions.stem: Why does the deck triple the word 'Assertions' on the testability checklist?

options.text: To emphasize how central assertions are to testable software
Because there are three assertion methods

questions.explanation: The deck repeats 'Assertions, Assertions, Assertions!!!' to stress their importance. (Software Testing p.14)

questions.stem: What does the deck mean by 'test ideas: make a list of new test ideas'?

options.text: Write tests only once at the end
Continuously capture test ideas to drive further TDD cycles

questions.explanation: The deck advises keeping a running list of new test ideas to feed TDD. (Software Testing p.40)

questions.stem: Why does the deck say to prefer specialised assertions in the queue/login tests?
questions.explanation: Specialised assertions (assertEquals, assertNull) give clearer failure messages. (Software Testing p.53)

questions.stem: What is the consequence of over-mocking (using a mock+verify where a stub would do)?

options.text: Tests become faster and more robust

questions.explanation: Over-mocking over-specifies the interaction, making tests fragile. (Software Testing p.59-60)

questions.stem: What does the deck's 'Real TDD' state require regarding test timing?

options.text: The test is written strictly before the implementation
The test is written after the code

questions.explanation: Real TDD writes the test strictly first. (Software Testing p.38)

questions.stem: Which is the correct description of a 'transition state' between traditional testing and TDD in the deck?

options.text: 'Moving to TDD' - an intermediate maturity between code-first and test-first

questions.explanation: The deck names a 'Moving to TDD' transition state between traditional and real TDD. (Software Testing p.37)

questions.stem: Which best states the purpose of a checkRep wrapper invoked from the test fixture?

options.text: To validate the representation invariant after every operation during tests

questions.explanation: The checkRep wrapper validates the invariant after each operation in tests. (Software Testing p.52)

questions.stem: What does 'Implement only as much code so that the test does not fail' enforce?

options.text: The TDD minimality discipline (no speculative code)

questions.explanation: This rule enforces TDD minimality: only enough code to pass. (Software Testing p.39)

questions.stem: Which describes the deck's 'Traditional Testing'?
questions.explanation: Traditional testing is code-first, test-after. (Software Testing p.36)

questions.stem: What is the purpose of @InjectMocks in the ArticleManagerTest?

options.text: To inject the @Mock/@Spy fields into the ArticleManager under test

questions.explanation: @InjectMocks wires the mocks/spies into the ArticleManager via constructor DI. (Software Testing p.74-75)

questions.stem: In the queue, why is the wrap-around boundary worth a dedicated white-box test?

options.text: The 'if (tail == max) tail = 0;' path is only hit at a specific boundary and could harbour a bug
Because it is a black-box concern

questions.explanation: The wrap-around path executes only at the boundary, so a dedicated test (testEnqueueTail) targets it. (Software Testing p.13)

questions.stem: What does the guide identify as the heartbeat of the Verification phase and CI?

options.text: Regression testing run on every commit

questions.explanation: Regression testing on every commit is the heartbeat of Verification/CI. (Software Testing p.81)

questions.stem: What is the best description of the 'Moving to TDD' state?

options.text: A transitional maturity between traditional and real TDD

questions.explanation: 'Moving to TDD' is the transitional state between traditional testing and real TDD. (Software Testing p.37)

questions.stem: Which assertion family member is best for confirming a boolean condition is false with a clear message?
questions.explanation: assertFalse is the specialised assertion for a false condition. (Software Testing p.53)
